raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Larger vehicles like SUVs are disproportionately likely to injure or kill pedestrians.",
    "Vehicles have been steadily increasing in size and weight since the 1980s.",
    "Today, some vehicle models weigh several thousand pounds.",
    "California has a higher pedestrian fatality rate than the national average.",
    "No state has more pedestrian deaths on its roadways than California.",
    "A study will be conducted to examine the relationship between vehicle size and injuries to vulnerable road users.",
    "The study will also assess the impact of a registration fee based on vehicle weight.",
    "Higher vehicle weight registration fees may influence drivers' behavior.",
    "Revenue from weight-based registration fees could be used to enhance safety features for pedestrians and cyclists.",
    "At least 13 states charge weight-based registration fees for vehicles.",
    "Collateral damage from collisions between larger vehicles and pedestrians is more likely to result in serious injury and death."
  ]
}
```
